name: Chronic Kidney Disease - Albuminuria (UACR) Lab Query
description: Tests ability to find chronic kidney disease patients via elevated urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR). The Columbia CKD V4.2 algorithm uses albuminuria for A-staging: A1 < 30 mg/g (normal), A2 30-300 mg/g (moderately increased), A3 > 300 mg/g (severely increased). The KDIGO 2012 guideline includes albuminuria as a kidney damage marker that can establish CKD even with normal eGFR (CKD Stage 1 by proteinuria). This test case uses UACR > 30 mg/g to capture A2 and A3 staging.
prompt: Find laboratory observations for urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) where the value is greater than 30 mg/g, indicating moderately or severely increased albuminuria.

prompts.broad: Find Observation resources for urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) where the value is greater than 30 mg/g creatinine. UACR > 30 mg/g defines KDIGO A2 (moderately increased albuminuria, 30-300 mg/g) or A3 (severely increased albuminuria, > 300 mg/g) — both indicate kidney damage and can establish CKD even with normal eGFR.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources for elevated UACR. Use LOINC 9318-7 (Albumin/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in Urine) — this is the standard random spot-urine UACR. Apply a value-quantity filter with the gt prefix at threshold 30. The query should look like: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|9318-7&value-quantity=gt30. The Columbia CKD algorithm also accepts LOINC 14959-1 (Microalbumin/Creatinine ratio) and 24-hour urine albumin (LOINC 1755-8, A24) but the spot UACR is most common.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated UACR (Paths A and D). The KDIGO 2012 framework uses albuminuria as an independent kidney damage marker. Path D patients have UACR > 30 mg/g but normal eGFR — this is CKD Stage 1 by proteinuria, often missed by eGFR-only screening.
